source_file: Radiology Patient record audit tool - NHQS version1   11 July 2025.xls

standards.statement: The practice respects the rights of patients and their families to refuse or discontinue treatment.

criteria.description: Patients are informed about their condition and the proposed investigation
The practice informs patients and their families about their rights to refuse or discontinue treatment, and the consequences of such decisions.

When patients/carers refuse or discontinue treatment, the consultation regarding their decision is accurately and contemporaneously recorded and includes the details of the discussion regarding the consequences of the decision

standards.statement: The practice has a clearly defined process for obtaining consent.

criteria.description: The practice has a documented policy outlining the procedure for obtaining general consent for treatment

The practice has a documented policy outlining the procedure for obtaining consent in the case of patients who are unable to grant consent for themselves by way of age or mental/physical incapacity, which is in accordance with the relevant laws and regulations
Documented consent is obtained from patients for health information to be provided to a third party
Where practice members use patient information for research, approval has been obtained from the relevant ethics committee and from patients themselves where required.

standards.statement: At registration, sufficient details are taken from the patient to ensure that the patient can be contacted by the practice when necessary and that the clinical team has sufficient background information to provide adequate care to patients.

criteria.description: New patients to the practice are asked to complete a form detailing at least a) – k) in the standard intent above.
Patients are asked to update the practice if their contact details change.
Health funder details are accurately recorded for each new patient when required

standards.statement: Patients are able to obtain advice or information related to their clinical care by telephone and electronic means.

criteria.description: The practice implements a policy on telephonic and electronic communication with patients that details at least a) – e) in the standard intent above.
There is evidence of practice/patient telephone or electronic advice and information in the patient health records.

standards.statement: Patient and family education promotes the concept of taking responsibility for one’s own health care.

criteria.description: The practice offers relevant health education to its patients in a planned and consistant manner which enables patiens to meet their ongoing health needs and achieve their health goals

Posters and pamphlets regarding high risk and commonly treated conditions relevant to the practice population (e.g. HIV, TB, Diabetes, Hypertension) are visibly displayed and accessible in the waiting room and routinely provided to patients during the consultation when appropriate.
Patient and family education provided is noted in the patient record.
There is documented evidence that patients are referred to these resources, where appropriate.

Prior to a diagnostic imaging procedure being rendered, except in cases of emergency, the practice must ensure that risks regarding the relevant procedure have been advised to the patient or substitute decision maker where necessary

standards.statement: Patient records contain the required information for the relevant condition

criteria.description: Notes for each consultation contain as a minimum points a)-k above
Hand written notes are legible
Notes are recorded contemporaneously
All abbreviations are standardised according to recognised local and national standards
The patients’ records, including contact details, are up to date to ensure the transfer of the latest information between care providers.
There is evidence of review where applicable of the results of procedures and diagnostic tests performed by a medical practitioner
Adverse drug reactions are noted in the patient's record

standards.statement: The diagnostic imaging practice must demostrate that the services provided are only undertaken where there is an identified clinincal need which is consistent with best available evidence

criteria.description: The clinical team uses current clinical guidelines relevant to the diagnosic imaging practices to assist in the diagnosis and management of patients
The clinical team can demostrate/describe how they ensure consistency in the preprocedre,imaging,reporting and recording processes of the diagnostic service
The clinical team can demonstrate how they communicate about clinical issues and support systems in the practice.

standards.statement: The diagnostic imaging practice must ensure that all patients are correctly identified and matched to their intended procedure or treatment

criteria.description: At least three (3) approved patient identifiers are used to match a patient to their request or medical record from the time the patient presents and through all stages of the diagnostic imaging service and when transferring responsibility of care.
Relevant personnel correctly match patients with their intended diagnostic imaging/therapeutic procedure
Relevant personnel correctly match patients with the correct anatomical site(if applicable) of the diagnostic imaging procedure

standards.statement: There is a process to ensure the safe and legal prescribing, administration and storage of medication

criteria.description: The person prescribing and dispensing the medicine has access to patient information that would contraindicate administration of particular medicines
Patients at risk from adverse reactions are identified according to practice policy
Medication is administered safely by a qualified individual and the effects of medication is actively monitored with all the relevant details recorded in the patient's records
There is a system/mechanism in place for reporting, investigating and responding to incidents arising from adverse reactions

standards.statement: The practice has a system for effective communication of clinical findings,follow up and review of tests and results

criteria.description: Patients health records contain evidence that all imaging reports and clinical correspondence received by the practice have been handled according to practice policy
There is a system to advice patients of the process for the follow up of results
A documented system to identify follow up and recall patients with clinically significant results is implemented
When patients are informed of the results over the telephone, the patient is identified with 3 approved identifiers before the information is given

standards.statement: There is a process for appropriate referral of patients for specialised consultation/investigations at other healthcare facilities

criteria.description: There is a documented process to refer patients
The lines of communication between the practice,referral centres and community services are clearly defined
Referrals are to specific individuals and or agencies in the patient's home community whereever possible
Patients and as appropriate are given follow up instructions which are provided in an understadable form and manner
A copy of the referral note is available in the patient record.
The referral is made on appropriate stationery or electronically.
The referral contains as a minimum a) – f) in the standard intent above
Follow-up care based on the findings of investigations/consultations performed outside the practice is noted in the patient record

standards.statement: The practice has an effective clinical handover system that ensures safe and continuing healthcare delivery for patients.

criteria.description: There is implementation of a documented policy on clinical handover to ensure that standard processes are followed
Patients share in decision making regarding the handing over of clinical information
Handover of clinical information is recorded in the patients' health records

Information relating to those patients requiring special attention, such as the chronically ill or those with infections which require specific infection control measures, is provided in a patient held letter, a copy of which is kept in the patient record

standards.statement: The practice ensures safe and reasonable arrangements for clinical care for patients outside of normal opening hours.

criteria.description: Patient health records contain reports or notes of investigations occurring outside normal opening hours by or on behalf of the practice

standards.statement: There are documented policies and procedures to guide personnel in all aspects of radiation safety within the practice

criteria.description: Policies and procedures that relate to limiting the irradiation of patients to levels consistent with medical requirements are implemented. The ALARA(as low as reasonable achievable) principle is applied to the calculation,preparation and administration of radioactive doses
A strict policy on the conditions under which pregnant women may be subjected to a procedure involving radiation exposure is available and implemented
Policies and procedres that relate to avoiding radioactive contamination and controlling spread should it occur are implemented
Policies and procedures are implemented for clinical trials where applicable
